item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm almost certain doing this to someone was a working strategy in at least one sims game.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Artist may terminate his employment at any time he chooses through direct verbal or written notice. In order to prevent fraudulent termination of this contract, notice through an intermediary will not be accepted.

Holy shit that's top-level loopholes right there.

As far as the article itself goes, I like it. Dark, sad; the tattoo he got after the second-to-last painting (his wife or daughter or someone?) really hits in the feels.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this is fucked up. Knew I was gonna be in for a ride when I read the first paragraph of the description. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have no mouth so I must paint. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fuck. +1.

Aside, 'tracking beacon' was comprehensible but a bit odd. Maybe 'tracking device' or, more robustly, 'any remote sensing means'?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh boy, Westhead Media and their brand of copyright-infringing art-slave-exploitation is back!

The Artist may terminate his employment at any time he chooses through direct verbal or written notice. In order to prevent fraudulent termination of this contract, notice through an intermediary will not be accepted.

Oh dearie me.

+1

(Someday, I'll write that SCP where an Anomaly made of Honey wipes out Westhead Media. It's gonna be a tribute)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really liked this one. The test logs and alterations element are a shade reminiscent of 191, but this tells a very different story, and I love the potential of the "monster" in this scenario. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 - I loved it when I read it and I'm glad to see it up!

(I'm a sucker for short and punchy skips, if you couldn't tell)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I loved the experiment log here. Originally I felt like ending it with repeated instances of drawings of it without modifications would have made sense, but the current one is much better.

I didn't like the inclusion of your GOI, but I can understand why you did that and I didn't let it affect my judgement of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Artist may terminate his employment at any time he chooses through direct verbal or written notice. In order to prevent fraudulent termination of this contract, notice through an intermediary will not be accepted.

oh that son of a bitch.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Thanks to Jak, Askedforit, werylium and Henzoid for their feedback!
